title: OEBVS Oregon Crypto Lead — Bradley outreach email (12/19/22)
html:
TL;DR

  • Cold-outreach draft to Jonathan Bradley (rga.com) about the Oregon Enterprise Blockchain Venture Studio — Yahir's first documented cold email into a real Oregon crypto-org.
  • Self-introduces as a 2nd-year OSU CS student, "interested in crypto and blockchain since 2018."
  • Asks two surgical questions: (1) is OEBVS still operating, (2) can he get more info.
  • Predates the Feb 2023 OSIG pivot doc by ~7 weeks — same energy, different target. Yahir was already prospecting outside OSIG before he wrote the explicit pivot.
  • Establishes the "scoured the internet for any organization doing cool things in crypto" posture — same active-search pattern that surfaces in the Corvallis Crypto Club founding arc.

Notes

Polished but short professional email. Names the org by acronym (OEBVS), asks if it's still alive — a giveaway that he found a stale page and is fishing. The Bradley address is jonathan.bradley@rga.com — preserved as it appears in the source's first line as the discovered contact, not as Yahir's personal contact info; flagged for redaction-review if it surfaces again. The 2018 self-dating of the crypto interest matches the crypto entry point timeline referenced elsewhere (corvallis crypto club arc starts 2022 but the personal interest is 4 years older). No reply documented. The OEBVS itself becomes a stub worthy entity — first appearance of an Oregon institutional blockchain effort in the corpus.

Connections

  • oebvs · corvallis crypto club · osu · crypto
  • 2023 02 10 osig going forward · osig · the austin thesis
  • cold email as prospecting · 2022 12 19 jonathan bradley rga
